Because unlike the rest of this building, the first level plays by a completely different set of rules, with over 3,000 square feet under air, and an additional 2,000 square feet of private patio space. While others are looking at the view, you are living it. This is the unit that hosts, the one that entertains, and the one that people gather around. And the inside, it is already done for you. Every detail was curated by the original owner to reflect a level of living that feels effortless, refined, and complete. It's being offered fully furnished, meaning the moment you take ownership, that lifestyle does not wait. It begins instantly. We are in Tarpen Point weekly, and the lifestyle it provides is resort quality. Met with the Ritz inspired design, and you are living the Florida lifestyle. And by the boating enthusiast out there. Tarpen Point, Marina, provides deep water access with quick passage to the river, and go. Contact the twins, Ariel and Haley Baptista. For any questions or interest. And don't forget to follow Twins' cell-south was Florida, for more Southwest Florida.
Cape Coral_FL.script:
What if you could own the only unit in Tarpon Point that literally rewrites the rules of waterfront living?

This is not a regular condo. This is 3,000 square feet under air plus 2,000 square feet of private patio inside Cape Coral's most iconic marina community. The elevator opens directly to YOUR floor. The views are not shared. The experience is not average.

And it comes fully furnished. Curated by the original owner down to every last detail. You walk in and the lifestyle just starts. No waiting. No decorating. No guessing.

For the boaters out there, Tarpon Point gives you deep water access with a straight shot to open water. Resort amenities. Ritz level design. Southwest Florida sunshine every single day.

This is the unit people talk about at dinner parties because they wish they owned it.

So here is my question for you. If money was not the issue, what would your dream waterfront lifestyle actually look like?

Cape Coral_FL.author: coastalluxuryliving
Cape Coral_FL.title: Cape Coral is one of the best-kept secrets in Southwest ...
Cape Coral_FL.transcript: Cape Coral is honestly a really special place to live. For people who love the Southwest for a lifestyle, water, sunshine, voting, relaxed space, it checks a lot of boxes. So here are some of the best things about Cape Coral as a town and for everyday living. First, the waterfront lifestyle is unmatched. Cape Coral has more than 400 miles of canals, which is actually more than venicellally. Many homes sit directly on the water and a lot of residents keep boats right in their backyard. From there, you can head out to the Clues to Hachi River, 4 out to the Gulf of America. You can go fishing, you can go enjoy a sunset cruise, kayaking, island hopping, as part of normal life here, which is a lot of fun. We do it all the time. For boaters, it's one of the easiest places in the country to live that kind of lifestyle. Another great thing is the quality of life and relaxed space. Cape Coral is a quieter, more suburban coastal city, also known for being clean, safe and family-friendly, with a laid-back feel where people spend a lot of time outdoors. A slower pace is one reason that so many retirees, families, and people that can work remote move here. Outdoor lifestyle is a huge draw to. Dozens of parks, nature areas, trails, waterfront spots for boating, biking, walking, fishing, Cape Harbor, the Weston, there's so many to name off. Why at life is also part of the daily life here. So you'll see dolphins, manatees, pelicans, other coastal wildlife, the burrowing owls, are huge here. And then there's a value for waterfront living, which is important. So compared to many other coastal cities in Florida, Cape Coral offers pretty affordable waterfront homes. In some cases, canal homes can cost significantly less. Comparable waterfront homes in places like Naples, Fort Myers Beach, Finita Springs, Santa Barbara Island. Also makes the boating lifestyle a little bit more accessible to buyers and homeowners. Cape Coral also has a strong community feel. Posts many farmers markets, festivals, outdoor concerts, community events throughout the year. Neighbours tend to be more residential and peaceful. Many residents know their neighbors, they'll have black parties, they'll all get together. Another advantageous location. So you're close to some of the best destinations such as Santa Bob Island, Captiva Island, Fort Myers Beach, Mount Lache, Pine Island, there's many to name off. Also having the access to our International Airport, which makes traveling pretty simple. I love our airport. You really don't have long lines. Living here is amazing. You get warm temperatures all year round. It means the boating, golfing, dining outdoors, beach days can happen almost any day of the year. If you're looking for more information on Cape Coral, please let us know.
Cape Coral_FL.script:
Cape Coral is lowkey one of the most underrated places to live in all of Florida and I say that as someone who actually lives here.

400 miles of canals. More than Venice. Boats in backyards. Dolphins on your morning kayak. This is just Tuesday here.

And here's the part nobody talks about enough. You can get a waterfront home here for WAY less than Naples or Fort Myers Beach. Like significantly less. The lifestyle is the same. The price tag is not.

Families love it. Retirees love it. Remote workers are flooding in right now because the quality of life just hits different. Clean, safe, outdoor everything, warm 365 days a year.

Farmers markets, Sanibel day trips, an airport with zero lines. Neighbors who actually know each other.

People who find this place usually never leave.

So real question for you. What's actually keeping you from making a move like this right now?

Naples_FL.caption:
Families don’t accidentally end up in Naples. 

They choose it.

Here’s what they’re actually choosing.

	1.	Year-round outdoor living — not the idea of it, the actual thing.

	2.	Neighborhoods where kids still roam and parents finally exhale.

	3.	Beach days at Clam Pass that stop being vacations and start being Tuesday.

	4.	Homes with room for grandparents, guests, and everyone staying longer than planned.

	5.	Schools that are excellent without being relentless.

	6.	Weekends that slow down. Dinners that actually happen.

	7.	A lifestyle that’s healthy because Pelican Bay, paddle boards, and the farmers market are just… there.

	8.	Neighbors in the same season of life — building something similar, together.

	9.	More time. With the people who matter most.

I moved here after living in New York, Chicago, and Newport Beach. I stayed for exactly this.

Families don’t move here for the house.

They move here for how life finally feels.

Which one resonates most? Drop the number below.

Follow @buylivesellnaples for real Naples life and real estate.

Cheena Chandra, a luxury real estate advisor selling Naples waterfront and coveted homes and neighborhoods that match how you want to live.

And behind this door, you have a walk-in pantry and a dining room off the kitchen, then office at the front of the home with a really large master bedroom and triple glass sliders to your pool. And down this hallway, you have a master bathroom with dual sinks at your vanity and a large soaking tub with a walk-in shower, a closet number one, a closet number two, bedroom number two, bedroom number three, bathroom number two, with 12 feet of glass sliders that lead to your covered linoid, complete with a pool with fantastic sunset views.
Punta Gorda_FL.script:
Here's your $800K in Punta Gorda and honestly it hits different in person.

You walk in through 10 foot doors into these soaring ceilings with natural light everywhere. The kitchen island is massive, walk in pantry right there, dining room flows perfectly. There's a dedicated office up front for whoever works from home.

The master suite though. Triple glass sliders straight to the pool, soaking tub, dual vanity, TWO closets. And the secondary bedrooms are genuinely big, not that fake big you see in listings.

12 feet of glass sliders open to the covered lanai and that pool with sunset views every single night. Like actually every night.

This is the kind of home people buy and never leave Punta Gorda.

So real question for you... if this showed up in your budget tomorrow, would you be ready to move on it or are you still waiting on something specific?

Bonita Springs_FL.caption:
Let’s talk about the different areas 👇🏼

Southwest Florida has tons of different lifestyles. The fun part is figuring out which one is for you. 

The MLS is saying this: 
• Lehigh Acres is the easiest point of entry with a median price of $315,000. It offers space, affordability, and a slower pace ideal for buyers looking to stretch their dollar 

• Cape Coral is the most affordable place to get the waterfront home you’re looking for. It’s laid back but has great growth with new restaurants and shopping coming. For people that like to fish or boat, this is where you’ll thrive. 

• Fort Myers has the best historic charm, riverfront views and is great for everyday livability. 

• Fort Myers Beach is a lifestyle revolving around sunsets and casual beach living. Beach bars, barefoot strolls and front-row sunset views. 

• Bonita Springs is the sweet spot between Naples and Ft Myers, offering relaxed coastal living with just enough buzz. They’ve got a great, up and coming restaurant scene too. 

• Estero is a polished, master-planned pocket where convenience, shopping and newer communities steal the spotlight. 

• Naples is luxury. High-end shopping, pristine beaches and a polished, resort-style every day lifestyle. 

• Marco Island is upscale island living with luxury homes and beautiful white-sand beaches. 

Thinking of moving to Southwest Florida and still trying to figure out which part is best for you? Reach out to me and we’ll chat through some options! 

#southwestfloridarealtor #capecoralrealtor #floridaliving

4 beds. 2 baths. Over 1900 square feet. Low 300s. And their mortgage is only $2,200 a month.

Here's how it happened. We locked them into a down payment assistance program that covered everything upfront. No repayment. Then negotiated $15,000 in seller concessions that wiped out every single closing cost.

Most people miss deals like this because they're googling answers at midnight instead of talking to someone who actually knows the programs available RIGHT NOW in Florida.

This isn't luck. This is strategy. And the window on these programs doesn't stay open forever.

Hey so I need to tell you something about Florida new construction homes that most agents won't say out loud...

Builders right now are basically PAYING you to buy. We're talking rate buydowns, closing cost credits, free upgrades. They have inventory sitting and they need to move it before quarter end.

I literally walked a client through a brand new 4 bedroom last week and the builder covered 15k in closing costs. She moved in with almost nothing out of pocket.

Florida is still one of the top states people are relocating to and these deals are not going to last once inventory tightens back up.

New construction also means no bidding wars, no inspection nightmares, no surprise repairs. You pick your finishes and you're done.

So real question for you... have you actually toured a new construction community in Florida yet or are you just watching from the sidelines?

USA.title: Curious how experts see the 2026 housing market shaping up ...
USA.script:
Here is the script:

Wait... experts are calling for a 14% jump in home sales in 2026 and most buyers have zero idea this is coming.

Here is what that actually means for YOU right now.

When mortgage rates drop toward 6%, millions of buyers who got priced out suddenly qualify again. All at once. That is not a slow trickle. History shows us these recoveries always happen fast and big, never slow and small.

So if you are sitting on the fence thinking you have all the time in the world... you might be planning your move right when everyone else is making theirs.

The people who win in real estate are the ones who move before the crowd figures it out.

I work with buyers in this market every single day and I can tell you exactly what this forecast means for your specific neighborhood.

So tell me... are you planning to buy or sell in the next 12 months?

USA.caption:
The 2026 housing market is already starting to feel different, especially in Long Beach and Orange County. Buyer motivation is returning in a meaningful way, and many people who have been waiting for the right moment are finally ready to make a move. This shift is happening alongside gradually improving interest rates and new leadership expected at the Fed this May, which has many watching closely for what comes next.

At the same time, more homes are expected to hit the market. Increased inventory means more choices, healthier negotiations, and a more balanced real estate environment than what we have seen over the past several years. Whether you are buying or selling, understanding these changes early can give you a real advantage.

If you are planning a move in 2026, now is the time to get informed and prepared. This is not the same market we have been navigating since 2020.

What are your predictions for the housing market this year? Drop a comment below.

Rick J. Lee
Realtor @ ΓEA⅃ Broker

USA.title: Rick J Lee | Long Beach & Orange County Realtor® on ...
USA.script:
Here's the truth nobody's talking about right now.

Everyone's obsessed with interest rates but that's actually NOT the biggest story heading into 2026.

What I'm seeing on the ground in Long Beach and Orange County is way more interesting.

Motivated buyers are coming back. Like actually motivated. That hasn't happened in years.

Rates are slowly coming down. New Fed leadership hits in May and insiders are already whispering about more cuts coming.

AND inventory is finally opening up. Real options. Real choices. The kind of market where you can actually negotiate again.

This is that window. The one people look back on and say "I wish I moved on that."

The buyers and sellers who act in the next 90 days are going to look like geniuses by end of year.

So tell me something... are you waiting for perfect or are you ready to actually make a move this year?

USA.caption:
2026 is shaping up to be a pivotal year in real estate. 🏡

@therealjasonabrams is watching three pressure points:

1️⃣ Interest rates: They jumped from 3% to nearly 8%. If they keep trending down, demand shifts.

2️⃣ First-time buyers: The average age is 40. If affordability improves, does the next wave step in?

3️⃣ Mortgage lock-ins: Millions sit on 3% rates. If rates ease, does inventory unlock?

What are you watching in 2026? Comment below.
USA.author: kellerwilliamsrealty
USA.title: 2026 is shaping up to be a pivotal year in real estate ...
USA.script:
2026 is about to shake up real estate in a BIG way and here are the 3 things you need to know right now.

Rates. They hit almost 8% back in 2023 and have been slowly dropping. More cuts could be coming this year. That matters for your wallet.

First time buyers. The average age just hit 40. FORTY. As the market softens that number should start dropping again. Meaning more competition for starter homes.

And the big one. Mortgage lock-in. Millions of homeowners are sitting on 3% rates feeling completely stuck. But life happens. New jobs. Babies. Divorces. And when rates drop enough those homes flood the market.

Here is what that means for YOU. More inventory. More opportunity. Possibly your best window in years to buy or sell.

So tell me honestly, are you waiting for rates to drop before you make your move?

Just listed this gem in Cedar Hills and honestly... I couldn't keep quiet about it 🏡

This neighborhood is one of those places where neighbors actually wave at each other. Kids ride bikes. People stay for DECADES. And right now one of the best houses on the block just hit the market.

We're talking updated kitchen, a backyard that's made for summer nights, and a price point that still makes sense in this market.

Here's the thing though. Houses in Cedar Hills don't sit. The last three I sold here were gone in under a week.

Comment "Cedar Hills" below and I'll DM you the full address, photos, and price before everyone else sees it.

But real talk... are you actually ready to stop scrolling and start packing boxes this year?
